name: playwright-triage
tagline: Triages Playwright GitHub bug reports: classifies issue type, reproduces across browsers/versions, and writes a self-contained regression.
verdict: Well-structured triage flow with clear classification logic, explicit failure handling (ToT vs reported version, incomplete repro), concrete report format, and a security-aware note to skim untrusted linked repos before running them. Weak spots: no stated exclusions from other triage-like tasks, no dedup check when dropping a repro test into tests/page/ on.

highlights.summary: `npm install @playwright/test@next` pulls the mutable @next tag of the official package for tip-of-tree testing — unpinned by necessity.
No 'NOT for' / exclusion text anywhere despite overlapping adjacent tasks (fixing bugs, general debugging).
'Drop it into the repo (`tests/page/`) and run it with `npm run ctest`' has no check for an existing file on re-triage of the same issue.
